senior_junior_culture.summary: The official JoSAA profile describes a senior-mentoring culture. Public student accounts support active cross-batch interaction through residential life and clubs, but independent evidence on consistency, hierarchy and fresher inclusion remains limited.
negative_themes: Experience may vary by hostel and group
Insufficient independent evidence for a college-wide conclusion

academic_culture.summary: Official sources describe a flexible interdisciplinary curriculum, thesis work and access to courses across departments. Student accounts describe strong peer learning and opportunities but also a demanding workload.
positive_themes: Flexible course choices and interdisciplinary exposure
negative_themes: Workload and grading experience can vary by course and instructor
Balancing coding, core academics and clubs may be demanding

hostel_reality.summary: Official sources state that all students are provided hostel rooms, with single occupancy, mess, LAN, laundry, gym and common facilities. Video and forum sources show substantial residential infrastructure, while mess preferences and block-level conditions vary.
positive_themes: Mess and daily facilities within hostels
negative_themes: Mess quality is subjective and mixed
Individual hostel blocks can differ in age and maintenance

club_and_campus_life.summary: IIT Guwahati has technical, cultural, sports and welfare boards, major festivals and numerous student clubs. Visual and student sources consistently emphasize the scenic residential campus and active events.
positive_themes: Wide technical and cultural club range
Major festivals including Alcheringa and Techniche
negative_themes: Recent dated activity is not available for every static-listed club
Club induction and workload can be selective or time-intensive

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official placement reports show high placement rates and compensation for CSE, Mathematics and Computing, ECE and DSAI, alongside active coding, analytics, robotics and electronics clubs. Software-role eligibility for each non-CSE branch remains employer-specific.
positive_themes: Strong placement outcomes in computing-related branches
Coding, robotics, analytics and electronics communities
negative_themes: Placement reports use registration denominators and CTC, not guaranteed take-home pay

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is scenic and self-contained on the north bank of the Brahmaputra, but about 22 km from central Guwahati. Institute buses and road transport provide connectivity; spontaneous city access takes planning.
positive_themes: Airport and railway access within roughly 30 minutes under normal conditions
Institute bus connection to city centre
negative_themes: Campus is separated from central city life
Traffic and evening transport conditions can extend travel time

overall_satisfaction.summary: Available evidence suggests a strong fit for students seeking a residential research university with a major peer network, technical opportunities and extensive campus life. Students prioritizing frequent urban access, uniform mess quality or guaranteed software eligibility across all branches should verify those trade-offs.
negative_themes: Limited independent evidence on some hostel and policy details
